title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: This test plan targets the post-silicon validation of Socket RAPL functionality at the highest feature hierarchy, focusing on its integration and operation within the SOC. Socket RAPL provides power and frequency limit enforcement across multiple domains (core, memory, etc.), interfacing with BIOS, TPMI, and various IPs. Key flows include BIOS initialization and locking of RAPL limits, TPMI-based domain enumeration and access, propagation of power/frequency ceilings from root to leaf IMHs and CBB dies, aggregation and real-time reporting of performance status, and coordinated enforcement via cross-domain PID controllers. The plan covers interactions between BIOS, TPMI, IMHs, CBB dies, and system-level status reporting, ensuring robust enforcement and accurate system state reflection under all operational and stress scenarios.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: The scope encompasses validation of BIOS and TPMI configuration integrity (including programming, locking, and domain enumeration), propagation and enforcement of power/frequency limits across all IPs, aggregation and reporting of LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_STATUS, stress and boundary testing (including negative scenarios and error handling), and cross-domain PID controller interactions. All flows from initialization to runtime enforcement, status reporting, and system response to invalid or extreme configurations are included. Out-of-scope items are any features not directly related to Socket RAPL, such as non-RAPL power management, unrelated BIOS/TPMI features, or IPs outside the RAPL domain.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: All features require access to BIOS configuration interfaces, TPMI registers, CSR access for RAPL limits and lock bits, and system-level monitoring tools for status registers. Hardware must support domain enumeration and real-time status reporting. Stress tools for generating extreme power/frequency demands and error injection capabilities are needed.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Potential security threats include unauthorized modification of RAPL limits post-lock, improper domain enumeration exposing sensitive configuration, and manipulation of status reporting. Mitigations involve strict enforcement of lock bits, access control on TPMI and CSR interfaces, and validation of error handling for invalid configuration attempts.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Risks include incomplete coverage of cross-domain interactions, undetected failures in lock enforcement, and inaccurate status aggregation under stress. Contingencies involve additional targeted testing for edge cases, enhanced monitoring during stress scenarios, and fallback to manual validation if automated tools fail.

title: Socket RAPL Feature Validation at SOC Integration Level
subsection content: Prerequisites include a fully functional BIOS with RAPL programming capability, TPMI register access, SOC hardware supporting all RAPL domains, and availability of stress and error injection tools. Initial domain mapping and lock bit configuration must be completed prior to test execution.

title: Limit Propagation & Enforcement Across IPs
subsection content: Verify correct propagation of power/frequency limits from root IMH to leaf IMHs and CBB dies. Test transitions when limits change, stress scenarios where IPs request frequencies above limits, and ensure throttling is enforced. Expected outcome: all IPs are constrained by the most restrictive domain, and frequency ceilings are respected.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: Validate correct propagation of dynamic Socket RAPL limits from root IMH to leaf IMHs and CBBs, ensuring frequency ceilings are enforced at all hierarchy levels.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: Dynamically reduce Socket RAPL PL1/PL2 limits via CSR/TPMI, randomizing order and magnitude, and monitor HPM messages and frequency responses at each hierarchy level.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: Randomize the order and magnitude of Socket RAPL limit changes, including values above and below TDP, down to fuse minimums.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: BIOS configures and locks CSR interface with TDP/EDP characteristics; Socket RAPL enabled; all CBBs and IMHs powered and operational.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages at all hierarchy levels; observe core and uncore frequencies; verify all domains are constrained by the most restrictive limit.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: Root IMH computes minimum of Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, and Platform RAPL PID outputs; propagates RAPL_PERF_LIMIT to leaf IMHs and CBBs; CBB Pcode sends LEAF_PERF_STATUS if any core is limited.

title: Limit Propagation from Root IMH to Leaf IMHs and CBBs under Dynamic Power Limit Changes
subsection content: BIOS locks CSR to prevent in-band writes; all programmed RAPL limits are critical and most restrictive is always enforced.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: Configure BIOS to lock CSR interface with TDP and EDP characteristics. Enable Socket RAPL and ensure all CBBs and IMHs are operational. Set initial Socket RAPL PL1 and PL2 limits above TDP. Dynamically reduce PL1/PL2 limits in steps, including values below TDP and down to fuse minimums, using CSR or TPMI. At each step, monitor RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages from root IMH to leaf IMHs and from leaf IMHs to CBBs. Observe core and uncore frequencies on all CBBs and sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and verify BIOS has locked CSR interface with TDP/EDP characteristics. 2. Enable Socket RAPL and confirm all CBBs and IMHs are powered and operational. 3. Set initial Socket RAPL PL1/PL2 limits above TDP via CSR/TPMI. 4. Randomly reduce PL1/PL2 limits in steps, including values below TDP and down to fuse minimums. 5. At each step, sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages at root IMH, leaf IMH, and CBB. 6. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages from CBBs. 7. Observe core and uncore frequencies on all CBBs. 8. Record and analyze results.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: Pass if at each step, all CBBs and IMHs receive the updated, most restrictive frequency limit as computed by root IMH. Core and uncore frequencies must not exceed the propagated limit. LEAF_PERF_STATUS must indicate throttling if requested frequencies exceed the limit. Fail if any domain exceeds the propagated limit or status messages are inconsistent.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: Confirm via HPM message logs, PMON, and frequency monitoring that limits are correctly propagated and enforced at all hierarchy levels.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for RAPL limit propagation and frequency enforcement.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: Use OSBV/SVOS (latest supported version), PMON (latest), Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, and Prime95 for workload generation. Map OS-level utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS version/config/knobs set for RAPL and CSR lock; OS kernel and scheduler settings for workload bursts; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers at validated versions; registry keys and OS overrides for power management.

title: Dynamic Limit Propagation Validation
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, and parameters; topology with all sockets, DIMMs, PCIe devices, and power rails; DIMM and PCIe device details; thermal solution as specified; debug instrumentation and power measurement setup.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: Randomize the order and magnitude of Socket RAPL PL1/PL2 limit changes, including values above and below TDP, and monitor the propagation of RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages. Ensure frequency ceilings are respected at all hierarchy levels.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: 1. Initialize platform with BIOS-locked CSR and enabled Socket RAPL. 2. Generate a randomized sequence of PL1/PL2 limit changes, covering values above TDP, below TDP, and down to fuse minimums. 3. Apply each limit change via CSR/TPMI. 4. At each step, sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages at root IMH, leaf IMH, and CBB. 5. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages from CBBs. 6. Observe core and uncore frequencies. 7. Analyze propagation and enforcement.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: Pass if all domains are constrained by the most restrictive limit at each step, and frequency ceilings are respected. Fail if any domain operates above the propagated limit or status messages are inconsistent.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to verify correct propagation and enforcement of limits.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for limit propagation and frequency enforcement.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and CSR lock; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Randomized Limit Change Order and Magnitude
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: At each dynamic limit change, verify that core and uncore frequencies on all CBBs do not exceed the propagated limit. Confirm LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages indicate throttling when requested frequencies exceed the limit.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: 1. Boot platform with BIOS-locked CSR and enabled Socket RAPL. 2. Set initial PL1/PL2 limits above TDP. 3. Dynamically reduce limits in steps, including values below TDP. 4. At each step, generate workloads to drive CBBs to request maximum frequencies. 5. Sample core and uncore frequencies and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages. 6. Analyze if frequencies are clipped to the propagated limit and status messages reflect throttling.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: Pass if frequencies are always clipped to the propagated limit and LEAF_PERF_STATUS indicates throttling when requests exceed the limit. Fail if frequencies exceed the limit or status messages are inconsistent.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm frequency ceiling enforcement and status consistency.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for frequency enforcement and status reporting.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and CSR lock; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Frequency Ceiling Enforcement and Status Consistency
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: Validate that IPs are throttled when requesting frequencies above the propagated RAPL_PERF_LIMIT, and status registers reflect correct limiting reason.

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: Generate workload bursts on CBBs to request frequencies above RAPL_PERF_LIMIT, monitor frequency clipping, LEAF_PERF_STATUS, and fast_throttle assertion.

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: Randomly vary number of CBBs and magnitude of frequency requests to cover different scenarios.

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, Platform RAPL enabled and configured; all CBBs and IMHs operational.

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: Monitor core and uncore frequency requests, actual frequencies, LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages, and IO_THERM_INDICATIONS.FAST_RAPL_THROTTLE[22].

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: RAPL_PERF_LIMIT propagated from root IMH to leaf IMHs and CBBs; CBB Pcode enforces throttling and updates LEAF_PERF_STATUS; fast_throttle asserted when limits are exceeded.

title: Throttling Enforcement When IPs Request Frequencies Above Propagated Limits
subsection content: Throttling enforced by clock division and architectural throttling (1:8); fast_throttle asserted when limit is below Pm.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: Configure Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, and Platform RAPL limits so that the computed frequency ceiling is below maximum turbo but above Pm. Generate workload bursts on selected CBBs to request core and uncore frequencies above RAPL_PERF_LIMIT. Monitor actual frequencies, LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages, and IO_THERM_INDICATIONS.FAST_RAPL_THROTTLE[22] for fast_throttle assertion.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and enable/configure Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, and Platform RAPL. 2. Set limits so frequency ceiling is below turbo but above Pm. 3. Generate workload bursts on selected CBBs to request frequencies above RAPL_PERF_LIMIT. 4. Monitor core and uncore frequency requests and actual operating frequencies. 5. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages from each CBB. 6. Sample IO_THERM_INDICATIONS.FAST_RAPL_THROTTLE[22]. 7. Record and analyze results.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: Pass if actual frequencies are clipped to the propagated limit, LEAF_PERF_STATUS increments for the corresponding limiter, and fast_throttle is asserted when limit is below Pm. Fail if frequencies exceed the limit or status registers are inconsistent.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm frequency clipping, status reporting, and fast_throttle assertion.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for throttling enforcement and fast_throttle assertion.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and throttling; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Frequency Request Above Limit Enforcement
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: Set RAPL limits so that the computed frequency ceiling is below Pm. Generate workloads to request frequencies above the limit. Monitor IO_THERM_INDICATIONS.FAST_RAPL_THROTTLE[22] for fast_throttle assertion and verify architectural throttling is triggered.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure RAPL limits so frequency ceiling is below Pm. 2. Generate workloads on CBBs to request frequencies above the limit. 3. Sample IO_THERM_INDICATIONS.FAST_RAPL_THROTTLE[22] and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages. 4. Observe core clock division and architectural throttling (1:8). 5. Record and analyze results.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: Pass if fast_throttle is asserted, architectural throttling is triggered, and LEAF_PERF_STATUS increments for the limiter. Fail if fast_throttle is not asserted or throttling is not enforced.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm fast_throttle assertion and architectural throttling.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for fast_throttle and architectural throttling.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and throttling; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Fast Throttle Assertion Below Pm
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: During workload bursts that request frequencies above the propagated limit, verify that LEAF_PERF_STATUS increments only for the active limiter and status registers reflect the correct limiting reason.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure RAPL limits. 2. Generate workload bursts on CBBs to request frequencies above the limit. 3. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages and IO_THERM_INDICATIONS.FAST_RAPL_THROTTLE[22]. 4. Observe status register updates and limiting reason. 5. Record and analyze results.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: Pass if LEAF_PERF_STATUS increments only for the active limiter and status registers reflect correct limiting reason. Fail if status registers are inconsistent or multiple limiters are reported simultaneously.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm status register consistency and correct limiting reason.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for status register consistency.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and throttling; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Status Register Consistency During Throttling
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: Validate consistent propagation of limit reason and status reporting across Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, Platform RAPL, and RACL, ensuring only one limiter bit is set and status fields update correctly.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: Program different combinations of limits, randomize which is most restrictive, generate workloads above the tightest limit, and monitor HPM messages and status fields.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: Randomize which limit is the tightest at each step and generate workloads to request frequencies above the limit.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, Platform RAPL, and RACL enabled; all IMHs and CBBs operational.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages at all hierarchy levels; observe limiter bit and status field updates.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: Root IMH and leaf IMH propagate minimum resolved limit and corresponding limiter bit; CBB Pcode updates LEAF_PERF_STATUS for active limiter.

title: Limit Reason Propagation and Status Reporting Consistency
subsection content: RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM message contains flag bitfields programmed in 1-hot manner; only one flag set at any time based on priority.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: Program different combinations of Socket RAPL, Platform RAPL, Fast RAPL, and RACL limits so that at various times, each is the most restrictive. At each configuration, generate workloads on CBBs to request frequencies above the tightest limit. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages at each hierarchy level and observe which limiter bit is set.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and enable/configure Socket RAPL, Platform RAPL, Fast RAPL, and RACL. 2. Program different combinations of limits, ensuring each is most restrictive at different times. 3. At each configuration, generate workloads on CBBs to request frequencies above the tightest limit. 4. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages at root IMH, leaf IMH, and CBB. 5. Observe which limiter bit is set. 6. Record and analyze results.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: Pass if at any time, only one limiter bit is set in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT (1-hot). Fail if multiple bits are set or limiter bit does not match the most restrictive limit.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm limiter bit consistency.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for limiter bit consistency.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and limiter priority; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Limiter Bit Consistency Validation
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: When the most restrictive limit changes, verify that the limiter bit and LEAF_PERF_STATUS field update accordingly. Generate workloads to request frequencies above the new limit and monitor status field increments.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure limits. 2. Change which limit is most restrictive by reprogramming limits. 3. Generate workloads to request frequencies above the new limit. 4. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages and LEAF_PERF_STATUS from CBBs and IMHs. 5. Observe status field updates. 6. Record and analyze results.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: Pass if limiter bit and status field update correctly when the most restrictive limit changes. Fail if status fields do not update or multiple limiters are reported.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm status field updates.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for status field updates.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and limiter priority; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Status Field Update on Limiter Change
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: At each configuration, verify that LEAF_PERF_STATUS increments only for the active limiter and no more than one perf status field counts up for the same duration.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure limits. 2. Program different combinations of limits. 3. Generate workloads to request frequencies above the tightest limit. 4. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages from CBBs and IMHs. 5. Observe which status field increments. 6. Record and analyze results.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: Pass if only one perf status field increments for the active limiter. Fail if multiple status fields increment or status reporting is inconsistent.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm active limiter status reporting.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for status reporting consistency.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider, Prime95. OS utilities for register checks and stress patterns.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and limiter priority; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Active Limiter Status Reporting
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: Validate that locked RAPL limits cannot be overridden by in-band agents and the system maintains the locked limit.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: Set and lock Socket RAPL limits via CSR, attempt to modify via in-band agent, and monitor HPM messages and status fields for changes.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: Not applicable; single negative test flow.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: BIOS configures and locks CSR interface with TDP/EDP characteristics; Socket RAPL enabled and running.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: Monitor RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages; verify no change occurs after override attempt.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: BIOS locks CSR; in-band agent attempts override; PCU algorithm enforces most restrictive limit.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: Lock bits set in CSR path; writes from locked source disabled; reads permitted.

title: Negative Test: Attempt to Override Locked RAPL Limit
subsection content: Attempt to override locked limit and verify system ignores the attempt.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: Configure BIOS to lock CSR interface with TDP and EDP characteristics. Enable Socket RAPL and set initial limits via CSR. Attempt to modify the RAPL limit via in-band agent (e.g., MSR write) while the lock bit is set. Keep power consumption constant. Monitor RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages and LEAF_PERF_STATUS from CBBs.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and verify BIOS has locked CSR interface with TDP/EDP characteristics. 2. Enable Socket RAPL and set initial limits via CSR. 3. Attempt to modify RAPL limit via in-band agent (MSR write) while lock bit is set. 4. Keep power consumption constant. 5. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages and LEAF_PERF_STATUS from CBBs. 6. Record and analyze results.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: Pass if no change in RAPL_PERF_LIMIT or LEAF_PERF_STATUS occurs and locked limit remains enforced. Fail if any change is observed or frequency/status changes occur.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm locked limit enforcement and absence of override.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for locked limit enforcement.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: Tool and Content Requirements: OSBV/SVOS (latest), PMON, Perspec path, PythonSV, Supercollider. OS utilities for register checks.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection content: Test Requirements: BIOS config/knobs for RAPL and CSR lock; OS kernel/scheduler settings; PCIe/graphics/storage drivers; registry keys and OS overrides.

title: Locked Limit Override Attempt
subsection title: Test HW Requirements and HW Configuration *
subsection content: Platform with correct SoC stepping, TDP, topology, DIMMs, PCIe devices, thermal solution, debug instrumentation, and power measurement setup.

title: Readback Consistency After Override Attempt
subsection content: After attempting to override the locked RAPL limit, read back the limit via CSR and MSR interfaces to confirm the value remains unchanged. Monitor HPM messages and status fields for consistency.

title: Readback Consistency After Override Attempt
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure locked CSR interface. 2. Attempt to modify RAPL limit via in-band agent. 3. Read back RAPL limit via CSR and MSR interfaces. 4. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages and LEAF_PERF_STATUS. 5. Record and analyze results.

title: Readback Consistency After Override Attempt
subsection content: Pass if readback values remain unchanged and HPM/status fields are consistent. Fail if any change is observed.

title: Readback Consistency After Override Attempt
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm readback consistency.

title: Readback Consistency After Override Attempt
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for readback consistency.

title: Readback Consistency After Override Attempt

title: Status Register Stability During Override Attempt
subsection content: During the override attempt, monitor LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages to verify that status registers remain stable and no frequency or status change occurs.

title: Status Register Stability During Override Attempt
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure locked CSR interface. 2. Attempt to modify RAPL limit via in-band agent. 3. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages before and after the attempt. 4. Record and analyze results.

title: Status Register Stability During Override Attempt
subsection content: Pass if status registers remain stable and no change occurs. Fail if any change is observed.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: Validate enforcement of the most restrictive limit during rapid and simultaneous updates to Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, Platform RAPL, and RACL, ensuring frequency ceilings and status registers are updated correctly.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: Randomly and rapidly update limits in overlapping sequences, generate workload bursts, and monitor HPM messages, status fields, and frequencies.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: Randomize and rapidly update limits for all domains; generate workloads requesting frequencies above current limits.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, Platform RAPL, and RACL enabled; all IMHs and CBBs operational.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT and LEAF_PERF_STATUS HPM messages at all hierarchy levels; observe core/uncore frequencies; verify no excursions above most restrictive limit.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: Root IMH and leaf IMH min-resolve limits from all domains; CBBs report status via LEAF_PERF_STATUS; frequency limits clipped to valid range set by fuses.

title: Stress Test: Rapid Transitions and Simultaneous Limit Updates
subsection content: RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages sent every 500us; most restrictive limit always enforced.

title: Rapid Limit Transition Enforcement
subsection content: Set initial limits for Socket RAPL, Fast RAPL, Platform RAPL, and RACL. Randomly and rapidly update limits in overlapping sequences, including cases where multiple limits become most restrictive in quick succession. Simultaneously generate workload bursts on CBBs to request frequencies above current limits. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages, LEAF_PERF_STATUS, and actual core/uncore frequencies.

title: Rapid Limit Transition Enforcement
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and enable/configure all limits. 2. Set initial limits for all domains. 3. Randomly and rapidly update limits via CSR/TPMI in overlapping sequences. 4. Simultaneously generate workload bursts on CBBs. 5. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages at root IMH, leaf IMH, and CBB. 6. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS from CBBs. 7. Observe core and uncore frequencies. 8. Record and analyze results.

title: Rapid Limit Transition Enforcement
subsection content: Pass if at all times, the most restrictive limit is enforced, frequency ceilings are updated, and status registers reflect correct limiting reason. Fail if frequency excursions above the most restrictive limit are observed or status registers are inconsistent.

title: Rapid Limit Transition Enforcement
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm enforcement during rapid transitions.

title: Rapid Limit Transition Enforcement
subsection content: Enable SST, Central, and Concurrency checkers. Enable PVT checkers for rapid transition enforcement.

subsection content: During simultaneous updates to multiple limits, verify that frequency ceilings are updated in accordance with the latest propagated limit and status registers reflect the correct limiting reason. Monitor for any frequency excursions above the most restrictive limit.

Generate workloads to request frequencies above current limits. 4. Sample RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages and LEAF_PERF_STATUS. 5. Observe core and uncore frequencies. 6. Record and analyze results.

title: Simultaneous Limit Update Consistency
subsection content: Pass if frequency ceilings are updated correctly and status registers reflect the correct limiting reason. Fail if frequency excursions or inconsistent status registers are observed.

title: Simultaneous Limit Update Consistency

subsection content: During rapid transitions and overlapping limit updates, monitor LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages to verify that status registers update in accordance with the latest propagated limit and no frequency excursions occur.

title: Status Register Update During Rapid Transitions
subsection content: 1. Boot platform and configure all limits. 2. Rapidly update limits in overlapping sequences. 3. Generate workloads to request frequencies above current limits. 4. Sample LEAF_PERF_STATUS and RAPL_PERF_LIMIT HPM messages. 5. Observe status register updates and frequency ceilings. 6. Record and analyze results.

title: Status Register Update During Rapid Transitions
subsection content: Pass if status registers update correctly and no frequency excursions occur. Fail if status registers are inconsistent or frequencies exceed the most restrictive limit.

title: Status Register Update During Rapid Transitions
subsection content: Use PMON, Visa, and HPM message logs to confirm status register updates during rapid transitions.
